reasoning: The market price for this forecasting question is 1% (0.01). While a NASA GISS release for May 2025 reported a global temperature anomaly of 1.07 °C (which is strictly less than 1.10 °C), recent years (2023-2024) have seen persistent anomalies significantly above 1.1 °C, and record temperatures globally. The highly confident 1% market probability reflects strong consensus that the October 2025 anomaly will climb back or remain above the 1.10 °C threshold (i.e. >= 110 in the GISTEMP table). I adjust slightly upwards (4%) to account for natural climate variability or a sharp La Niña cooling, which the May 1.07 °C anomaly hints might be possible, but defer heavily to the strong market prior.
